---
title: "Web knowledge scales the pattern"
---

## Outcome

Attendees understand why a web source becomes a durable knowledge base when a single file is too static or too narrow.

:::: {.visual-grid}
::: {.visual-panel}
### Use a public page when

- the information already lives on the web
- updates happen outside the class repo
- attendees need a realistic grounding example
:::

::: {.visual-panel}
### Watch for these issues

- navigation clutter
- content that is too promotional
- pages with weak factual structure
:::
::::

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A(Public page) --> B(Index content)
    B --> C(Create knowledge source)
    C --> D(Test grounded questions)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Design principle

Pick pages with stable factual content, not flashy landing pages. The cleaner the page, the easier it is to explain the result.
:::

## Good demo prompts

- What does this page say about the service scope
- Summarize the key facts in five bullets
- What information is still missing for a customer decision
